title: Class 2 multi-turn refinement — refinement turn timeout escalates to caregiver
description: Deterministic scenario for the doc 11 multi-turn refinement long-tail path. Same opt-in policy (class2_multi_turn_enabled=true). The user picks C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE on the parent turn opening a refinement session. The user then DOES NOT respond within class2_refinement_turn_timeout_ms. The manager calls handle_timeout on the refinement session producing a terminal Class2Result with transition_target=SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION. The audit record carries refinement_history with the unanswered turn (selection_source='timeout' / synthetic 'TIMEOUT' selected_candidate_id). silence ≠ consent invariant preserved across refinement-turn timeout. Scenario asset only — does not authorize actuation.

steps.description: Publish an insufficient-context payload that drives the policy router into Class 2.
Class2ClarificationManager.start_session initialises the parent session with C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE included in candidate_choices.
User picks C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE. submit_selection_or_refine returns a refinement ClarificationSession (because flag is on AND template exists).
TTS announces the refinement question and two per-room choices.
The user provides no input within class2_refinement_turn_timeout_ms (default 30000ms). The Mac mini's refinement-turn waiter expires.

Manager.handle_timeout(session=refinement_session, trigger_id='C207') returns a terminal Class2Result. transition_target='SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION'. The terminal record's refinement_history captures the unanswered turn — _build_record reads selection_result (which carries the synthetic timeout marker) and produces a refinement_history entry with selected_candidate_id reflecting the timeout.

Publish the terminal clarification record on safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. refinement_history is populated with the unanswered turn so audit can tell 'the user picked the parent but never picked a refinement' apart from 'the user never picked anything'.
The terminal SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION result triggers the caregiver notification path. The caregiver may still confirm a CLASS_1 action via Telegram inline keyboard if appropriate.

Observe the audit stream and capture: parent selection, refinement session creation, refinement turn timeout, caregiver escalation. The integration verifier asserts (a) refinement_history length=1 with the timed-out turn, (b) terminal transition is SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, (c) caregiver notification was published.

transition_outcomes.condition: User picks parent C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE then does not respond to the refinement turn within class2_refinement_turn_timeout_ms.
transition_outcomes.example_confirmation: C1_LIGHTING_ASSISTANCE on parent, no response on refinement turn

notes: Refinement-turn timeout follows the same silence ≠ consent invariant as initial-turn timeout: the unanswered turn never auto-confirms a candidate, only escalates.

refinement_history is recorded even on the timeout path so audit can distinguish 'user picked parent but didn't refine' from 'user never picked anything' (the latter would be a parent-session timeout escalation without any refinement_history entry).
The caregiver may still override via Telegram inline keyboard — caregiver Phase 2 mechanics are shared with direct-select via _run_caregiver_phase (PR #109).
If the caregiver also fails to respond, the standard caregiver-no-response notification path produces a final SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION terminal — never autonomous execution.
